entries.reasoning: Only 1 of 10 named targets (111) appears in the ground-truth top-5% set, indicating poor identification of systemically at-risk protocols. While the report cites specific exposure figures and scenario losses and shows reasonable narrative awareness of April 2022 Terra/LUNC stress, the targeting accuracy is weak and several cited metrics appear unverifiable against the provided data.

Only 1 of 10 named targets (1413) appears in the ground-truth top-5% set, indicating poor predictive accuracy, though the report cites specific metrics and offers plausible reasoning (LUNC/Terra collapse timing, USDT centrality).

The report cites specific scenario losses, distressed protocol counts, and exposure weights, and correctly identifies protocol 111 from the ground truth set while flagging the prescient LUNC/Terra tail risk. However, only one of its named targets (111) overlaps with the ground truth top-5% set, limiting precision despite strong analytical grounding.

The report cites specific exposure metrics and multiple scenario analyses, and correctly identifies protocol 111 which appears in the ground-truth top-5% set. However, most of its named targets (937, 118, 114, stablecoins) do not appear in the ground truth, limiting its precision on the actual systemic-loss leaders.

The report cites specific metrics (exposure values, scenario losses) and astutely identifies the Terra/LUNC collapse timing, which is highly relevant for this date; it correctly flags protocol 114 from the ground truth top-5% set, though most other named targets don't match the ground truth list, limiting precision.

The report cites specific metrics and provides a coherent narrative grounded in the UST/LUNA context, but none of its named target protocols (937, 2, WBTC, 118, 1571, 359, 111) match the ground-truth top-5% systemic risk set (1052, 1136, 117, etc.), indicating poor identification of the actual high-risk protocols.

The report cites specific metrics and scenario losses, but only 1 of its 9 named targets (protocol 118) appears in the ground-truth top-5% systemic set, indicating poor predictive accuracy on the actual high-loss protocols.

The report cites specific scenario metrics and identifies node 111 (Lending) which appears in the ground truth top-5% set, and contextualizes well with the June 2022 Terra/LUNA aftermath. However, most named targets (556, 866, WETH, 118, 2, WBTC, 937) do not match the ground truth systemic risk set, indicating the concentration-based methodology only partially captures the actual weight-loss leaders.

The report cites specific metrics (loss percentages, exposure weights, affected protocol counts) and correctly identifies protocol 111 as a key systemic risk, which appears in the ground-truth top-5% set. However, most named targets (WETH, 118, 2, 182, WBTC, USDT, 1599) don't match the ground-truth list, lowering precision despite strong analytical grounding and contextual awareness of the LUNA/UST collapse.

Only protocol 111 overlaps with the ground-truth top-5% set; most named targets (937, 240, USDT, USDC, etc.) are not among the high-loss protocols, so the report misidentifies the actual systemic-risk nodes despite offering specific-sounding metrics and reasonable post-Terra context.

The report correctly identifies protocols 118 and 111 from the ground truth top-5% set and provides specific metrics (exposure weights, stress scenario losses, affected protocol counts) with actionable supervisory recommendations, though most of its named targets (937, 2, WBTC, USDT, etc.) fall outside the ground truth set.

The report cites specific stress scenario metrics and exposure figures, and correctly identifies two ground-truth targets (111 and 118), but misses the majority of the top-5% systemic protocols and relies on named assets (USDC, WBTC, USDT) not confirmed in the ground truth, suggesting partial accuracy.

The report cites specific exposure figures and scenario losses with plausible contagion reasoning, but its named targets (WETH, USDT, 182, 240, 118, 111, 2) show essentially no overlap with the ground-truth top-5% protocols (1048, 1061, 1065, 1087, 114, 115, 117, etc.), indicating it largely misses the systemically-relevant set.

The report cites specific stress-scenario metrics and exposure weights, but its identified target protocols (WETH, USDC, 182, 118, etc.) show essentially no overlap with the ground-truth top-5% set by weight loss (1002, 114, 1207, 121, etc.), indicating it largely missed the actual systemic risk protocols.

The report cites specific scenario metrics and exposure figures, but its identified targets (937, USDT, 612, WBTC, etc.) do not overlap with any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic protocols (1036, 1061, 111, 115, etc.), indicating the risk ranking misses the actual high-loss protocols.

The report correctly identifies the timely and contextually relevant Terra collapse and provides specific exposure figures and stress-scenario metrics, but its named targets (USTC, LUNC, 294, USDT, etc.) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% protocols by weight loss, indicating weak alignment with the actual systemic-risk set.

Only 1 of 9 targets (118) matches the ground-truth top-5% set, indicating poor predictive accuracy, and the rationale relies heavily on scenario labels and dollar figures not verifiable from the provided data summary. While the Terra/UST contextualization is reasonable, the overall identification of systemic-loss protocols largely misses the mark.
